conditions: When grapes are grown in regions where growing season mean temperatures are below ~16–17°C. Effect is most pronounced for malic acid component; tartaric acid is temperature-independent.
explanation: Low growing temperatures slow the enzymatic degradation of malic acid during ripening. Malic acid is consumed during normal grape ripening via respiration; slower ripening means more malic acid is retained at harvest. Tartaric acid is stable regardless of temperature. The net result is higher total acidity and lower pH in cool-climate wines compared to warm-climate wines of the same variety at equivalent ripeness.
